title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 23, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Restaurants From an expansive Albanian newcomer with stuffed pies to an upcoming bar and restaurant based on the Seven Deadly Sins, here are the latest Boston restaurant openings, dining news, and food events to know right now.
Sunny Girl is opening an outpost in South Boston this summer.
Handout/Kevin Eduard White ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Gloucester woman helps rescue young driver from submerged car
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The mother rushed into action after spotting the driver panicking as their SUV sank into the water off Rocky Neck Avenue on Wednesday.
A 20-year-old woman was on her way to work when she pulled over into a parking lot off Rocky Neck Avenue on Wednesday morning.
But as she went to move, she hit the gas pedal instead of the brake, launching her car into the water.

title: 8 must-see acts at Newport Folk Fest this weekend
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Music Your guide to the fest, complete with last-minute changes and under-the-radar stars.

Among the artists gracing the Newport Folk Festival stage this weekend are (clockwise from top left) Brittany Davis, Father John Misty, Lauryn Hill, Cat Power, Jason Narducy and Michael Shannon, and Brandi Carlile.
Kurland Agency; Amy Harris/Invision/AP; AP Photo/John Locher; Elio Lee Hazel; Christy Bush; Rob Grabowski/Invision/AP Newport Folk Fest is Mother, as the kids say.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s MLS return in a draw vs. Toronto FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution A return to the turf, good and bad luck, and aside about socks.
Carles Gil during the Revolution's 0-0 draw with Toronto at Gillette Stadium on July 22.

AP Photo/Mark Stockwell By Hayden Bird July 23, 2026 | 9:39 AM 3 minutes to read The Revolution returned to MLS action for the first time in 60 days in a 0-0 draw against Toronto FC at Gillette Stadium on Wednesday.

title: Ruggles Station is getting Commuter Rail fare gates in August
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Transportation This will be the third MBTA station to have fare gates within the last four years.
The MBTA Ruggles Station will begin using fare gates for the Commuter Rail in August.
Pat Greenhouse/The Boston Globe Commuter Rail fare gates at Ruggles Station will begin operating Aug.

title: Red Sox’ franchise record-tying 15 game win streak ends with 5-1 loss to Orioles
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Boston Red Sox It was Boston’s first loss since a 10-2 setback to Washington at Fenway Park on July 1.
The Red Sox' Jarren Duran heads to the dugout after flying out to end the game.

AP Photo/Charles Krupa BOSTON (AP) — The Baltimore Orioles ended Boston’s franchise record-tying 15-game winning streak behind Kyle Bradish’s six innings of one-run ball, defeating the Red Sox 5-1 in the nightcap of a doubleheader on Wednesday.

title: Former Miss Hall’s head of school indicted for allegedly failing to act on knowledge that teacher was abusing students
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Prosecutors say the former Head of School received "numerous reports" about Matthew Rutledge's alleged inappropriate conduct with students.
Melissa Fares, 32, and Hilary Simon, 37, say they were preyed upon by history teacher Matthew Rutledge when they were students at Miss Hall's School in Pittsfield.
Jennifer S.

title: AG: N.H. apartment fire that killed 2 was set intentionally by 1 of the victims
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Local News The man believed to have set the fire had previously been charged with arson, officials said.
Two men were killed in a fire at a Nashua, New Hampshire apartment building Tuesday, including a man who appears to have set the fire intentionally in order to die by suicide, authorities announced.

In the early morning of July 21, the Nashua fire and police departments responded to a fire inside an apartment building on Orange Street, the office of Attorney General John Formella said in a press release on behalf of New Hampshire State Fire Marshal Sean Toomey, Nashua Police Chief Kevin Rourke, and Nashua Fire Chief Steve Buxton.

title: Suspect in fatal Boston Common assault arrested after second attack, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The suspect allegedly attacked a victim with a metal pole in downtown Boston after fatally assaulting another.
Ten police vehicles and over a dozen law enforcement personnel remained on scene at Boston Common, where a man was found with severe injuries early Wednesday.

Bryan Hecht for The Boston Globe A person accused of fatally assaulting a man near Boston Common early Wednesday was arrested hours later after allegedly attacking a second victim with a metal pole in downtown Boston, police said.
